title: SAN JOSE, CA – Senator Dave Cortese (D-San Jose) issued the following statement regarding his Senate Bill 483 (SB 483) advancing to the Governor for his signing and adoption.
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: SB 483 bans the archaic and deadly practice of prone restraint from being used in California schools.
The U.S. Department of Education has recommended banning the use of prone restraint.
Prone restraints are used in schools at a disproportionate rate against students with disabilities and students of color.
Prone restraints have harmed and even killed students.
SB 483 will encourage schools to identify, adopt, and implement evidence-based rehabilitative intervention and prevention strategies.
Students in California will not have to deal with the indignity and trauma of prone restraint due to SB 483.
